title: git SHAs as first-class substrate nodes — pros/cons and recommendation
question: Should git SHAs become first-class substrate items (a commit block kind) so commit references become edges, or stay as prose annotations on existing fields — judged by how it would functionally improve our ability to use the substrate?
method: Conceptual analysis of the two options against pi-context's operation surface (find-references, join-blocks, context-validate, requires-edge invariants, lenses, render).
findings_summary: Framed by which substrate operations it unlocks, the gain collapses to a single capability class: commit-as-pivot. A SHA in a prose field is opaque to every substrate operation (find-references, join-blocks, context-validate, invariants, lenses all see a string). Making commits first-class would add: reverse lookup (find-references(commit) → every DEC/DISC/US/invocation/log item that cites it, in one edge query, vs a prose grep); join-blocks across the commit edge; enforceable commit-edge invariants (a vivified story MUST have a vivified_by_commit edge whose target exists, not merely a non-null field); dangling-ref integrity (context-validate flags a ref to an unrecorded commit); and provenance renderable from the graph. The cost is equally concentrated: forward provenance ('what commit vivified US-1?') needs no node — it is just reading the field; the commit item DUPLICATES git (subject/author/date/diff all answered by git show/log) and adds nothing git lacks except the edges TO project state; and a hand- or fully-mirrored commit block is a git mirror that drifts — the exact population/sync anti-pattern this whole effort has been eliminating, with the integrity benefit only as good as the sync discipline. For this project's actual query patterns (pivot on DEC/DISC/US entities; SHA used as one-directional evidence), the marquee reverse-lookup is low-demand while the population cost is constant — a poor trade as a default. RESOLUTION: do not choose between rich-mirror and prose-only. If adopted, do it as a DERIVED commit kind — mint a commit item only for SHAs already cited in the prose fields (refs, resolved_by, vivifying_commit, sha), id = SHA, no mirrored metadata, regenerable from those fields — so it is a projection of references the substrate already holds, not an authored git mirror; this captures the pivot + integrity pros and dissolves the drift con (same generate-from-the-source discipline as the model-surface-authority thread). The prose stays on the field verbatim; the commit node + edges are the additive, regenerable overlay. RECOMMENDATION: prose-by-default now; promote commits to first-class only when a real need to pivot on commits or enforce commit-edge invariants appears, and only as a derived-from-cited-SHAs kind, never as a maintained git mirror.
